source_rationale: ADIPOQ (adiponectin) is the canonical adipokine secreted exclusively by mature adipocytes
PLIN1 (perilipin-1) coats lipid droplets in mature adipocytes and is a definitive adipocyte marker
PPARG is the master transcription factor driving adipocyte differentiation and lipid metabolism
FABP4 is a fatty acid binding protein highly expressed in adipocytes, essential for lipid transport
Lipoprotein lipase is a key enzyme in adipocyte lipid uptake from circulating lipoproteins
Hormone-sensitive lipase mediates lipolysis in adipocytes
PNPLA2 (ATGL) is the rate-limiting lipase for triglyceride hydrolysis in adipocytes
CD36 is a fatty acid translocase critical for long-chain fatty acid uptake in adipocytes
Aquaporin 7 is an adipocyte-specific glycerol channel important for lipid metabolism
CIDEC promotes lipid droplet enlargement and is characteristic of mature white adipocytes
Glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase is a key enzyme in adipocyte glycerolipid synthesis
FOS is an immediate-early transcription factor indicating an activated/responsive adipocyte state
TWIST1 is a transcription factor involved in adipocyte lineage regulation and brown fat programming
Annexin A1 is upregulated in this adipocyte subtype, involved in anti-inflammatory signaling
COL15A1 marks this adipocyte subpopulation with extracellular matrix remodeling activity
FKBP5 is a glucocorticoid-responsive gene marking stress-responsive adipocytes
TBX15 is a transcription factor that defines regional adipocyte identity
FABP4 is highly upregulated in this adipocyte subtype, marking mature lipid-laden adipocytes
ALDH1A1 marks visceral-type adipocytes and is involved in retinaldehyde metabolism

ADH1B is an alcohol dehydrogenase enriched in mature adipocytes
